evidences.title: Get historical klines from binance api - GitHub
evidences.content: #### CLI Options | Option | Alias | Description | --- | `--pair` | `-p` | Trading pair (e.g., BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT) | | `--interval` | `-i` | Kline interval (1m, 3m, 5m, 15m, 30m, 1h, 2h, 4h, 6h, 8h, 12h, 1d, 3d, 1w) | | `--start` | `-s` | Start date (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO 8601) | | `--end` | `-e` | End date (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO 8601) | | `--output` | `-o` | Output directory path (filename is auto-generated) | | `--format` | `-f` | Output format (json or csv, default: json) | | `--help` | `-h` | Display help | | `--version` | `-V` | Display version | #### Hybrid mode You can also provide some options and be prompted for the rest: ``` # You will be prompted for start date, end date, and output path # ``` ### Library [...] ## Installation ### Via Homebrew (macOS/Linux) ``` brew install maxgfr/tap/binance-historical ``` ### Via npm ``` npm install -g binance-historical ``` ### Download binary Download the latest binary for your platform from the releases page. ## Usage ### CLI #### Interactive mode ``` binance-historical download ``` #### Non-interactive mode Pass all options as arguments: ``` binance-historical download \ --pair BTCUSDT \ --interval 4h \ --start 2024-01-01 \ --end 2024-12-3...

evidences.content: `symbol` and `limit` are straightforward, `startTime` and `endTime` we now have covered by the above `date_to_milliseconds` function. We note from this that we can get a maximum of 500 results each request, so we will need to build a loop if we are fetching over a long time period. `interval` we have a list of so these are passed easily. The get\_klines endpoint returns an array of klines in this order ``` [1499040000000, # Open time "0.01634790", # Open "0.80000000", # High "0.01575800", # Low "0.01577100", # Close "148976.11427815", # Volume 1499644799999, # Close time "2434.19055334", # Quote asset volume 308, # Number of trades "1756.87402397", # Taker buy base asset volume "28.46694368", # Taker buy quote asset volume "17928899.62484339" # Ignore ] ``` [...] First install the `dateparser` package ``` install ``` Now we can create a function to take a readable string into milliseconds. With this function we can convert all sorts of handy date formats to milliseconds like the examples below. ``` print(date_to_milliseconds("January 01, 2018")) print(date_to_milliseconds("11 hours ago UTC")) print(date_to_milliseconds("now UTC")) ``` ### Binance Kline endpoint Now we have that...

evidences.title: Binance.US API Documentation
evidences.content: Data Source: Memory ### Get Candlestick Data > Example ``` -X "GET"" ``` ``` import requests resp = requests. get(' print(resp. json()) ``` > Response ``` ``` `GET /api/v3/klines` Use this endpoint to get Kline/candlestick bars for a token symbol. Klines are uniquely identified by their open time. Please note the maximum limit is 1,000 bars. Weight: 1 Parameters: | Name | Type | Mandatory | Description | --- --- | | symbol | STRING | YES | | interval | ENUM | YES | | startTime | LONG | NO | | endTime | LONG | NO | | limit | INT | NO | Default 500; max 1000 | If startTime and endTime are not sent, the most recent klines are returned. Data Source: Database ## Price Data ### Get Live Ticker Price > Example [...] + Previously some requests failed if the `startTime` == `endTime`. + Now, all API requests that accept `startTime` and `endTime` allow the parameters to be equal. This applies to the following requests: - `GET /api/v3/aggTrades` - `GET /api/v3/klines` - `GET /api/v3/allOrderList` - `GET /api/v3/allOrders` - `GET /api/v3/myTrades` Changes to Filter Evaluation: + Previous behavior: `LOT_SIZE` and `MARKET_LOT_SIZE` required that (`quantity` - `minQty`) % `stepSize` == 0. + New...

evidences.content: The URL below shows the changes on the price of the pair `YFI/USDT` with the minimum price change that the endpoint `api/v1/klines` can provide: `&interval=1m`. `YFI/USDT` `api/v1/klines` `&interval=1m` I looked through all the Binance API for around 2 hours tonight and I couldn't find a way to get data inferior to 1 minute. I wish I could find something like this: where `&interval=1s` mean `1 second`. `&interval=1s` `1 second` Using the Binance API, is there a way to get this information? Brian Tompsett - 汤莱恩's user avatar graffiti75's user avatar ## 2 Answers 2 According to the Binance API documentation, there is no chart interval lower than `1 minute` for klines: [...] `1 minute` `Kline/Candlestick chart intervals: m -> minutes; h -> hours; d -> days; w -> weeks; M -> months 1m 3m 5m 15m 30m 1h 2h 4h 6h 8h 12h 1d 3d 1w 1M` One possibility could be to fetch old trades and then generate an `OHLC(V)` table on your own. `OHLC(V)` fbardos's user avatar ## Comments Add a comment Updated answer as of 2025-05-04. evidences.content: By design, 99.99B XRP will be created. As of now, the circulating supply of XRP is approximately 61,227,830,000. All the prices listed on this page are obtained from Bitget, a reliable source. It is crucial to rely on a single source to check your investments, as values may vary among different sellers. Our historical XRP price dataset includes data at intervals of 1 minute, 1 day, 1 week, and 1 month (open/high/low/close/volume). These datasets have undergone rigorous testing to ensure consistency, completeness, and accuracy. They are specifically designed for trade simulation and backtesting purposes, readily available for free download, and updated in real-time. ## XRP Historical Data Use Cases Here are some of the ways XRP historical data is used in XRP trading: [...] ## About XRP Price History Data XRP price history tracking allows crypto investors to easily monitor the performance of their investment. You can conveniently track the opening value, high, and close for XRP over time, as well as the trade volume. Additionally, you can instantly view the daily change as a percentage, making it effortless to identify days with significant fluctuations. According to our XRP price...
